summary: All six acceptance checks pass. --version and -V both print '1.0.0' with exit 0, short-circuit before any side effects, no files are touched, usage text shows the new flags, shellcheck reports no warnings, and validate.sh passes all tests. Implementation exactly matches the analyst plan.
tests: bash install.sh --version --engine-repository foo/bar: prints '1.0.0' and exits 0 (short-circuits before processing other args)
find check before/after --version run: no files created, modified, or deleted
shellcheck install.sh .agent/scripts/*.sh: passes with no warnings, exit 0
bash .agent/scripts/validate.sh: all tests pass (Specialized pipeline, Installer, Engine config), exit 0
acceptance_checks: bash install.sh --version prints version string and exits 0: pass
bash install.sh -V behaves same as --version: pass
--version creates/modifies/deletes no files and performs no gh/git network operations: pass
